title: what is the definition of wetland?

messages.content: what is the definition of wetland?

According to the National Wetland Policy, a wetland is defined as: "areas of marsh, fen, peatland or waters, whether still or flowing, permanently or periodically wet to a depth of at least 60 centimeters" [National Wetland Policy, p. 3]. Additionally, the policy elaborates that this includes "areas of vegetation, soil, or water that are inundated or saturated by surface or ground water at a frequency high enough to support, and that under normal circumstances do support, a prevalence of vegetation typically adapted for life in saturated soil conditions" [National Wetland Policy, p. 3]. While the Fauna and Flora Protection Ordinance offers a simpler definition – "any marsh, swamp, bog, fen, or other area where the soil is saturated or inundated by surface or ground water" [Fauna and Flora Protection Ordinance, p. 15] – the National Wetland Policy provides a more comprehensive and current definition.
